title: Live executor seam and resume-live handoff
purpose: Define the single seam between durability (AD) and the engine (AE): the LiveExecutor trait that AE implements to actually run an activity, start a timer, await a signal, or spawn a child. The handoff rule is the load-bearing invariant — the LiveExecutor is invoked only after the resume point, never during replay — and after a live action its resulting event is recorded through the Recorder. This is how replay performs no side effect and live execution is cleanly separated.
task: Define the LiveExecutor trait (executor.rs) with one operation per world-touching command family. Implement the handoff glue: a function that, given a Command and a Resolver, returns the recorded Resolution when the cursor satisfies it, and otherwise (ResumeLive) invokes the LiveExecutor for that command and records the resulting event(s) through the Recorder, producing a live Resolution. The invariant is that the LiveExecutor is never touched while the cursor can satisfy a command. Test with a recording mock LiveExecutor over InMemoryStore that asserts zero live calls during replay and exactly the expected live calls after resume.

requirements.title: LiveExecutor trait
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a LiveExecutor trait, implemented by the engine (AE), with one async operation per world-touching family: run an activity, start a timer, await a signal, and spawn a child workflow. Each operation SHALL return the live outcome that AD will both return and record.
acceptance: executor.rs defines trait LiveExecutor: Send + Sync with methods run_activity, start_timer, await_signal, spawn_child taking the relevant Command payload and returning the live outcome (Payload / fired / signal payload / child result or error).
The trait is object-safe (usable as &dyn LiveExecutor) or generic — pick one and document it; the choice must let AE supply a beamr-backed implementation without AD depending on beamr.
AD provides no production implementation of LiveExecutor (that is AE's); it provides only the trait and a test mock.
Doc comments state the AD/AE boundary: AD calls this only on resume-live.

requirements.title: No live call while the cursor can satisfy a command
requirements.spec: WHILE the HistoryCursor can satisfy a Command (the Resolver returns a recorded Resolution), THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT invoke any LiveExecutor operation.
acceptance: The handoff function returns the recorded Resolution from the Resolver without touching the LiveExecutor when the cursor matches.
A test replays a fully-recorded history through the handoff with a mock LiveExecutor that panics if any method is called, and the replay completes with zero panics (proving no live call) (CO11).
The test asserts each returned Resolution equals the recorded outcome.

requirements.title: Resume-live handoff records the live outcome
requirements.spec: WHEN the Resolver returns ResumeLive for a Command, THE SYSTEM SHALL invoke the corresponding LiveExecutor operation and SHALL record the resulting event(s) through the Recorder, then return the live outcome as a Resolution.
acceptance: On ResumeLive for RunActivity, the handoff calls LiveExecutor::run_activity, records ActivityScheduled (if not already), ActivityStarted/Completed (or Failed) via the Recorder, and returns the live Resolution.
The events recorded on the live path are appended via the Recorder (single-writer, correct expected_seq) — no direct EventStore::append.

A test replays a partial history to its resume point, then drives one live RunActivity with a mock LiveExecutor returning a known Payload, and asserts: the LiveExecutor was called once, the new ActivityCompleted is in the store at the next sequence, and the returned Resolution carries the live Payload.
Re-reading the store after the live step and replaying again returns the now-recorded outcome from cache with zero further live calls (proving the live result was durably recorded).

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement a real (beamr-backed) LiveExecutor — that is AE; AD provides the trait and a test mock only.
SHALL NOT execute timers/signals/children's live semantics itself — the trait delegates to AE/AT; AD only orchestrates the call-and-record handoff.
SHALL NOT append events outside the Recorder (CO6).
verification: cargo test -p aion passes (handoff tests with the mock LiveExecutor).
A test proves zero LiveExecutor calls during replay of a fully-recorded history (CO11).
No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.
